Off Calendar May-17-2023 Continued To Aug-23-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances; Ronald Berman appearing via zoom for Kenneth Schwartz (Administrator).

Mr. Berman stated that he has seen the tentative/examiners notes and expressed that the examiner does not understand the difference between an acknowledgement and a jurat. The Court stated that Violet's estate needs to be sorted out. The First and final account etc. is continued to August 23, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Berman will get Violet's petition in as soon as possible and is to look over the affidavits. Mr. Berman stated that he will be filing a supplement explaining what a jurat and an affidavit should be.

Judge; Stephen Murphy; Clerk: Ateesh Datt; Deputy: Cristobal; Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ronald Berman appeared by telephone for Petitioner.

The Court stated that the issue is whether there is clear and convincing evidence that the document was intended to be the Decedent's will, whether there was sufficient diligence in tracking relatives and potential heirs, that the story seems to have changed as to who the parents are and talked about errors that were made initially. Mr. Berman stated that he believes the third supplement adequately indicates that there are no next of kin, that the second supplement addresses Probate Code 6110, stated that there is no other evidence to be brought, that it is the sum total, and that everyone has died and no one can verify it. The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-19-2019 Continued To Dec-17-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-22-2019 Continued To Nov-19-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-25-2019 Continued To Oct-22-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ronald Berman appeared by telephone for proposed Administrator.

The Court noted that the will is not in the Decedent's hand writing. Mr. Berman stated that there was a declaration some years ago from a Lady that took dictation and wrote the will, that the Lady passed away, that there were no witnesses and that they are stuck with what they got. The Court stated that if there is no legal authority that supports admission of the will, Mr. Berman can submit on what he has and the Court will make a determination. Mr. Berman stated that he has found nothing in case law in California or out of California, that there is $180,000.00 that still has to be probated, that there are no relatives of the Decedent and that there are relatives of the two Beneficiaries. The Court noted that more than likely the will will not succeed and this is headed towards intestacy, and that Mr. Berman can amend his petition to request letters of administration without a will. Mr. Berman requested a month continuance. The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is continued to October 22, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Berman is instructed to provide courtesy copies of all of the pleadings to the Court. The Court expects that the petition will be amended, or supplemented to provide information regarding diligence to look for additional Heirs.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
